---
author: "Ryan Taylor"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%x')`"
output: 
  html_document: 
    theme: paper
    highlight: haddock
    code_folding: hide
    toc: yes
    toc_float: yes
header-includes:
params: 
  set_title: "1/2025"
title: "`r paste('Multivariate Regression Analysis:', params$set_title)`"
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

# Hide warnings and messages in output;
# Align images in center of page
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  fig.align = "center",
  fig.height = 5,
  fig.width = 7,
  include = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  results = 'markup'
)
# Clear memory
rm(list = ls()); gc()

# Set universal options
options(scipen = 999, # Don't use scientific notation
        knitr.kable.NA = "" # Hide NA's in printed tables
)

# Load packages
if(!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(
  
  # Extras
  pwr, gtools, coda, numDeriv, mvtnorm, extraDistr, rstan, refund, tidyfun, 
  latex2exp, pROC, glmnet, corrplot, ncvreg, readxl, sf, MASS,
  table1, gtsummary, ggridges, forestplot, mgcv,
  broom.helpers, broom.mixed, patchwork, colorRamps, modelsummary, itsadug,
  
  # Staples
  DescTools, tidyverse, magrittr, janitor, glue,
  SASmarkdown, knitr, kableExtra, ggpubr, ggpmisc, stargazer
)

# Locate SAS for SAS chunks
saspath <- r"(C:\Program Files\SASHome\x86\SASFoundation\9.4\sas.exe)"
sasopts <- "-nosplash -ls 75"

# Make it easier to turn answers into rounded text
PrintNum <- function(x, acc = 0.01, percent = F, vec = F){
  if(percent){ 
    y <- scales::label_percent(accuracy = acc)(x)
  } else {
    y <- scales::label_number(accuracy = acc)(x)
  }
  if(vec)( y <- paste0("(",
                       paste(y, collapse = ", "),
                       ")"))
  return(y)
}

# knitr::opts_chunk$set(engine="saspdf", engine.path=saspath)

# knitr::opts_chunk$set(engine="R", engine.path=NULL)
# *** Use and include chunk header ', engine = "R"'

# setwd()

IMAGES_FOLDER <- here::here("analysis/Presentation Images")

```

# Overview

This document records the methods and results of a multivariate regression analysis on the Volumetric Craniosynostosis data.

## Updates
* Recalculated variance of bicoronal coefficient in explanatory model
* Changed simple model CV predictions
  + Model with first / only visits uses one observation per patient
  + Also predicted same model on same data as other models
* Excluded single left coronal observation from CV data
* Updated CV prediction square error summaries
  + Summarized square error distributions by suture type and by model (aggregating across all regions)
  + Plotted errors by region by model, colored by suture type
* Plotted examples of good and bad predictions on brain shapes
  + Averaged square errors across regions for a given patient and model; selected best and worst prediction for each suture type; plotted all models' predictions for that patient
* Some conclusions based on brain shape plots:
  + TE model seems to perform well when volumes are very abnormal (i.e., it may be incorporating information about how abnormal that patient is from the previous visit)
  + Simple models miss abnormalities (i.e., predictions use no previous information, so they are based on general patterns for a given suture type)
  + The combo model does not seem to perform as well on aggregate as the TE model estimating the effect of the previous measurement on each region separately
  
## Still to do
* Conduct F tests on predictors

# Questions for the team

## Internal

## Collaborators

* How is age incorporated into the personalized normative reference head volume? The paper potentially suggests it is incorporated as a quadratic term?
  + Why was PCA used to calculate normative reference head volume? Are these based on / do they line up with anatomical regions?
  + When in the process are volumes projected? Are they converted back to volumes in order to calculate the volume differences?

# Load and clean data

First we load the data for the analysis. All of the data is stored in the "Volumetric Results-8-6-2024" Excel file.

We create a clean column indicating the category of suture that we will use in our categorical predictor variable. If a patient has both left coronal and right coronal sutures, we classify them as "Bicoronal." If they have another combination of multiple sutures, we classify them as "multiple," and eventually exclude them. If they have no sutures, we classify them as "No suture." Otherwise, we classify them based on the single suture they have.

```{r load data}
here::i_am("analysis/2024.10_multivariate_regression.Rmd")

# File saved in "data" folder one level above repo
cranio_raw <- read_xlsx(here::here("../data/VolumetricResults-8-6-2024.xlsx"))

# Rename for easier coding
cranio <- cranio_raw %>% clean_names() %>% 
  rename(head_vol = head_volume_m_l,
         ref_head_vol = personalized_normative_reference_head_volume_m_l,
         age = age_at_image_days) %>% 
  rename_with(.cols = matches("volume_difference"), 
              ~str_replace(., "volume_difference_m_l", "vol_dev") %>% 
                str_replace("region", "reg"))

# Categorize each patient separately based on sutures 
# - (creating "bicoronal" / "multiple")
sut <- cranio %>% select(matches("fused")) %>% 
  mutate(i = 1:n()) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = -i) %>%
  mutate(suture = if_else(value == 1, name, NA_character_)) %>% 
  group_by(i) %>% 
  summarize(sutures = paste(na.omit(suture), collapse = "/")) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  arrange(i)

# Merge suture classification into original dataset
cranio %<>% 
  bind_cols(sut %>% select(sutures)) %>% 
  mutate(num_sutures = rowSums(select(., matches("fused"))),
         suture_type = case_when(num_sutures = 2 & 
                                   left_coronal_fused == 1 & 
                                   right_coronal_fused == 1 ~ "bicoronal",
                                 num_sutures > 1 ~ "multiple",
                                 num_sutures == 0 ~ "no_suture",
                                 T ~ sutures)) %>% 
  mutate(suture_type = str_remove_all(suture_type, "_fused"))
```

# Summaries for data cleaning

Our data's observations are at the image level. Our first task is to summarize the frequency of one individual patient having multiple pictures. We use the "UID" column to identify individual patients.

## Data counts

```{r data summ}

# Summarize counts of pictures and patients
cran_summ <- cranio %>% 
  summarize("Rows" = n(),
            "Unique Pictures" = n_distinct(image_identifier),
            "Unique Patients" = n_distinct(uid)) %>% 
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "  ", values_to = " ")

# Print summary table
summ_kable <- kable(cran_summ, caption = "All pictures and patients") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)

save_kable(summ_kable, paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/all_summ.html"))

webshot2::webshot(url = paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/all_summ.html"),
                  file = paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/all_summ.png"))

summ_kable
```

## Repeat visits

```{r mult}
# Summarize instances of multiple pictures for one patient
multi_pic <- cranio %>% 
  arrange(uid, age) %>% 
  group_by(uid, suture_type) %>% 
  mutate(days_diff = age - lag(age, 1)) %>% 
  summarize(pics = n(),
            avg_day_diff = mean(days_diff, na.rm = T)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  filter(pics > 1) %>% 
  group_by(suture_type) %>% 
  summarize(patients = n_distinct(uid),
            max_pics = max(pics),
            avg_pics = mean(pics)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(pct_data = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.01)(patients / cran_summ$` `[3])) %>% 
  arrange(desc(patients))

# Display table
kable(multi_pic, caption = "Multiple Pictures", 
      col.names = c("Suture Fusion", "Patients", "Max Pics / Patient", "Avg Pics / Patient", "% of Data"),
      digits = 2) %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)
```

## Suture types

Next, we summarize the frequency of different suture types in the data. We also summarize the specific types of multiple sutures, and investigate whether there are any patients whose diagnosis changes over time.

```{r sutures}

# Summarize frequency of suture types
sut_type <- cranio %>% 
  group_by(suture_type) %>% 
  summarize(pics = n(),
            patients = n_distinct(uid)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  arrange(desc(pics)) %>% 
  mutate(pats_included = if_else(suture_type %in% c("multiple", "right_lambdoid", "left_lambdoid"), 0, patients),
         pct_pats = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)(patients / sum(patients)),
         pct_included = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)(pats_included / sum(pats_included))) %>% 
  adorn_totals("row") %>% 
  select(-pats_included)

sut_kable <- kable(sut_type, caption = "Suture Representation", digits = 2,
      col.names = c("Suture Type", "Pictures", "Patients", "% of Patients", "% of Included")) %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)

sut_kable
```

## Age distributions

Finally, we summarize the distribution of age in the dataset. Over half of the data is from patients less than 1 year old, so we limit our analysis to these patients.

```{r age dist}

# Graph age distribution
cranio %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = age)) +
  geom_density(fill = "blue", alpha = 0.25) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0,3650, 365), limits = c(0, max(cranio$age)))

# Graph percent of data under chosen cutoff of {1 year}
cranio %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = age)) +
  geom_step(stat = "ecdf") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 365, color = "blue") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = ecdf(cranio$age)(365), color = "blue") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0,3650, 365), limits = c(0, max(cranio$age)))
```

# Data cleaning

Based on these summaries, there are some rare categories of data that we should exclude to reduce the variability of the data.

We use one image from each patient, which we choose as the last image taken for each patient. We also exclude multiple sutures and lambdoid sutures. Finally, one patient's sex is missing, so we exclude that patient.

```{r head devs}

# Calculate volume deviations across entire head
cranio %<>%
  mutate(vol_dev_sum = rowSums(select(., matches("reg_"))),
         vol_dev_head = head_vol - ref_head_vol)

```

```{r reg data}

# Filter to regression subset:
# - Exclude lambdoid
# - Age less than 1 year
# - No missing sex or age
cranio_sub <- cranio %>% 
  # Exclude rare sutures
  filter(!suture_type %in% c("multiple", "left_lambdoid", "right_lambdoid") &
           age <= 365 &
           !is.na(pt_sex) & !is.na(age))

# Format for cross-sectional multivariate reg
cranio_reg <- cranio_sub %>% 
  # Filter to one picture per patient
  arrange(uid, age) %>% 
  group_by(uid) %>% 
  # Use last picture taken for each patient
  slice_tail(n=1) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  # Code suture type as factor based on frequency
  mutate(suture_type = factor(suture_type, levels = setdiff(sut_type$suture_type, "Total")))

```

```{r reg data summ}
# Suture types in regression data

sut_type_reg <- cranio_reg %>% 
  group_by(suture_type) %>% 
  summarize(pics = n(),
            patients = n_distinct(uid)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  arrange(desc(pics)) %>% 
  mutate(pats_included = if_else(suture_type %in% c("multiple", "right_lambdoid", "left_lambdoid"), 0, patients),
         pct_pats = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)(patients / sum(patients)),
         pct_included = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)(pats_included / sum(pats_included))) %>% 
  adorn_totals("row") %>% 
  select(-pats_included)

sut_kable_reg <- kable(sut_type_reg, caption = "Suture Representation", digits = 2,
      col.names = c("Suture Type", "Pictures", "Patients", "% of Patients", "% of Included")) %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)

save_kable(sut_kable_reg, paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/sut_summ.html"))

webshot2::webshot(url = paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/sut_summ.html"),
                  file = paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/sut_summ.png"))

sut_kable_reg
```


# Data investigation

With this clean data, we perform some summaries to ensure that we understand the preprocessing on the data.

## Total volume deviation

First, we calculate the sum of volume differences for each patient across all 12 regions and compare this to the difference between the head volume and personalized normative reference head volume.

* Suppose $\boldsymbol{Y}_i = (Y^{(1)}_i, \ Y_i^{(1)}, \ ..., \ Y_i^{(12)})$ is the $12 \times 1$ vector of region-specific volume differences for patient $i$; $V^{(A)}_i$ is patient $i$'s actual overall head volume, and $V^{(R)}_i$ is the volume of the personalized reference head volume for patient $i$. We expected:
  + $\sum_{j=1}^{12} Y_i^{(j)} = V_i^{(A)} - V_i^{(R)}$ for each patient
  + I.e. the sum of the region-specific volume differences is equal to the overall head volume minus the personalized reference head volume

*We learned from collaborators that the reference head volume includes fewer regions than the actual head volume, so there is an explanation for why these do not match up.*

```{r head volume check}

# Compare sum of region devs to overall head dev
ggplot(cranio_reg, aes(x = vol_dev_head, y = vol_dev_sum)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = suture_type), alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "blue") +
  stat_poly_eq() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  labs(x = TeX("$V^{(A)} - V^{(R)}$"),
       y = TeX("$\\sum_J Y^{(J)}$"))
```

# Clean Summary statistics

## Table 1

With the cleaned data, we start with a Table 1 displaying information about the infants in our sample.

```{r table 1}
# Create table 1
demo_table <- table1::table1(~ 
                               image_type + pt_sex + pt_race + pt_ethnicity + 
                               age + head_vol + 
                               ref_head_vol | suture_type,
                             data = cranio_reg)

demo_table
```

## Outcome summaries

Next, we summarize the dependent variables: volume differences by region.

```{r vol ridge}

# Summarize 12 regions' outcomes as ridge plot
vol_ridge <- cranio_reg %>% 
  select(matches("reg_")) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = everything()) %>% 
  mutate(region_num = str_extract(name, "[0-9]+") %>% as.numeric() %>% as.factor()) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=value, y=region_num, fill=region_num)) +
  geom_density_ridges() +
  labs(title = "Volume Deviation Distributions")

# Summarize 12 regions' outcomes as jittered boxplot to show how many outliers there are
vol_box <- cranio_reg %>% 
  select(matches("reg_")) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = everything()) %>% 
  mutate(region_num = str_extract(name, "[0-9]+") %>% as.numeric() %>% as.factor()) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=value, y=region_num, fill=region_num)) +
  geom_jitter(width=0.1, alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Volume Deviation Distributions")

vol_ridge | vol_box
```

## Age against outcomes

Then, we investigate how age could be associated with region volume differences. 

```{r age plot, fig.dim = c(10, 8)}

# Scatterplot: age vs overall head volume
age_hv <- cranio_reg %>% 
  filter(age > 0) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = age, y = head_vol)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = suture_type), alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_smooth(method = "gam", formula = y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")) +
  stat_cor(digits = 2, p.accuracy = 0.001) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  labs(title = "Age vs Overall Volume",
       x = "Age (days)",
       y = TeX("$V^{(A)}$ (mL)"))

# Scatterplot: Age vs reference volume
age_rv <- cranio_reg %>% 
  filter(age > 0) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = age, y = ref_head_vol)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = suture_type), alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_smooth(method = "gam", formula = y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")) +
  stat_cor(digits = 2, p.accuracy = 0.001) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  labs(title = "Age vs Overall Reference Volume",
       x = "Age (days)",
       y = TeX("$V^{(R)}$ (mL)"))

# Scatterplot: age vs Overall Difference

age_diff <- cranio_reg %>% 
  filter(age > 0) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = age, y = vol_dev_head)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = suture_type), alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_smooth(method = "gam", formula = y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")) +
  stat_cor(digits = 2, p.accuracy = 0.001) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  labs(title = "Age vs Overall - Reference Volume",
       x = "Age (days)",
       y = TeX("$V^{(A)} - V^{(R)}$ (mL)"))

# Scatterplot: Overall Volume vs Reference Volume
vol_comp <- cranio_reg %>% 
  filter(age > 0) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = head_vol, y = ref_head_vol)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = suture_type), alpha = 0.25) +
  stat_cor(digits = 2, p.accuracy = 0.001) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  labs(title = "Actual Volume vs Overall Reference Volume",
       x = TeX("$V^{(A)}$ (mL)"),
       y = TeX("$V^{(R)}$ (mL)"))

# Scatterplot: Age vs volume differences
age_y <- cranio_reg %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = c(matches("reg_"))) %>% 
  filter(age > 0) %>% 
  mutate(region_num = str_extract(name, "[0-9]+") %>% as.numeric()) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = age, y = value)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = suture_type), alpha = 0.25) +
  stat_cor(digits = 2, p.accuracy = 0.001) +
  facet_wrap(~region_num) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  labs(title = "Age vs Region Volume Differences",
       x = "Age (days)",
       y = TeX("$Y_i^{(j)}$ (mL)"))

# Display these
(age_hv | age_rv) /
  (age_diff | vol_comp)

age_y
```

# Explanatory Regression

Next, we run a multivariate regression to find the association between the 12 regions' volume differences in a given patient with that patient's suture type.

```{r reg}

# Create list of suture type vars
cranio_fusion_vars <- cranio_reg %>% 
  select(matches("_fused")) %>% 
  select(-matches("lambdoid")) %>% 
  names()

# Preserve means and sd's for consistent normalization
cranio_scale <- cranio_reg %>% 
  summarize(across(c(head_vol, vol_dev_head, age),
                   .fns = list("mean" = ~mean(., na.rm = T),
                               "sd" = ~sd(., na.rm = T))))

# Normalize variables
cranio_reg %<>%
  mutate(across(c(head_vol, vol_dev_head, age),
                ~ ( . - cranio_scale[[paste0(cur_column(), "_mean")]][1]) / 
                  cranio_scale[[paste0(cur_column(), "_sd")]][1],
                .names = "{.col}_std"))

# Create regression formula: 
# => bind 12 outcome vars ~ Suture types (inc. bicoronal interaction) & 
#                           Overall head volume dev & Image type & Sex & Age
reg_form <- formula(paste("cbind(",
                          paste(
                            paste0("reg_", 1:12, "_vol_dev"),
                            collapse = ", "),
                          ") ~ ", 
                          paste(cranio_fusion_vars, collapse = " + "), 
                          " + right_coronal_fused * left_coronal_fused",
                          " + vol_dev_head_std + image_type + pt_sex + age_std"))

demo_form <- formula(paste("cbind(",
                          paste(
                            paste0("reg_", 1:12, "_vol_dev"),
                            collapse = ", "),
                          ") ~ ", 
                          # paste(cranio_fusion_vars, collapse = " + "), 
                          # " + right_coronal_fused * left_coronal_fused",
                          "vol_dev_head_std + image_type + pt_sex + age_std"))

# Run regression
vol_reg <- lm(reg_form, data = cranio_reg)

demo_reg <- lm(demo_form, data = cranio_reg)
```

The results from this model are presented in the table below.

```{r beta table}

# Save regression result in table
beta_tbl <- broom::tidy(vol_reg)

# Clean var-covar matrix for variance estimates
beta_cov <- vcov(vol_reg) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column() %>% 
  separate_wider_delim(rowname, ":", 
                       names = c("row_outcome", "row_var"), 
                       too_many = "merge") %>% 
  pivot_longer(-c(row_outcome, row_var), 
               names_to = "colname") %>% 
  separate_wider_delim(colname, ":", 
                       names = c("col_outcome", "col_var"),
                       too_many = "merge")

# Calculate bicoronal combination variance
vol_vcov <- vcov(vol_reg)

# Identify coronal variables in each region
coronal_ptrn_vec <- paste0("reg_", 1:12, "_.*coronal")

# Create several vectors with 1's in the place of coronal vars
bicoronal_c <- map(coronal_ptrn_vec,
                   ~as.numeric(str_detect(colnames(vol_vcov), .x)))

# Multiply contrast vectors with voc matrix to get combined variance
bicoronal_var <- tibble(
  response = paste0("reg_", 1:12, "_vol_dev")
) %>% 
  mutate(var = map_dbl(bicoronal_c, 
                       ~t(.) %*% vol_vcov %*% .))
         
# Add better bicoronal interpretation (combining first order and interaction)
bicoronal_tbl <- beta_tbl %>% 
  filter(str_detect(term, "coronal")) %>% 
  group_by(response) %>% 
  summarize(estimate = sum(estimate)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  # Join variance of all coronal-related terms
  # Q: Accurate std error?
  left_join(bicoronal_var, by = "response") %>% 
  mutate(std.error = sqrt(var)) %>% 
  mutate(statistic = estimate / std.error,
         p.value = pnorm(-abs(statistic)),
         term = "bicoronal") %>% 
  select(-var)

# Create full coefficients table
coeff_tbl <- beta_tbl %>% 
  filter(str_detect(term, "fused")) %>% 
  bind_rows(bicoronal_tbl) %>% 
  bind_rows(beta_tbl %>% filter(!str_detect(term, "fused"))) %>% 
  mutate(region_num = str_extract(response, "[0-9]+") %>% 
           as.numeric()) %>% 
  arrange(region_num)

# Extract RSE and R Squared measures for all 12 regions
lm_stats <- distinct(coeff_tbl, region_num) %>% 
  mutate(rse = purrr::map_dbl(1:length(summary(vol_reg)),
                              ~summary(vol_reg)[.][[1]]$sigma),
         r_2 = purrr::map_dbl(1:length(summary(vol_reg)),
                              ~summary(vol_reg)[.][[1]]$r.squared),
         adj_r_2 = purrr::map_dbl(1:length(summary(vol_reg)),
                                  ~summary(vol_reg)[.][[1]]$adj.r.squared),
         adj_r_2_demo = purrr::map_dbl(1:length(summary(demo_reg)),
                                  ~summary(demo_reg)[.][[1]]$adj.r.squared))

# Merge coefficients and R squared for models; calculate confidence intervals
coeff_tbl %<>% left_join(lm_stats) %>% 
  select(response, rse, r_2, adj_r_2, everything()) %>% 
  mutate(low_95 = estimate - 1.96*std.error,
         high_95 = estimate + 1.96*std.error,
         region_num = str_extract(response, "[0-9]+") %>% as.numeric(),
         clean_var = str_remove_all(term, "suture_type"),
         term_name = paste0(region_num, ": ", clean_var))

# Display using kable, coloring background of each column to show best predictions
lm_stats_k <- lm_stats %>% 
  select(region_num, matches("adj_r_2"))

r_2_range <- lm_stats %>% 
  select(matches("adj_r_2")) %>% 
  pivot_longer(everything()) %$% 
  unique(value)

lm_stats_k$adj_r_2 <- cell_spec(round(lm_stats$adj_r_2, 3), color = "black", bold = T, 
                              background = spec_color(lm_stats$adj_r_2, option = "turbo", 
                                                  scale_from = c(min(r_2_range), max(r_2_range)),
                                                  begin = 0.2, end = 0.8))

lm_stats_k$adj_r_2_demo <- cell_spec(round(lm_stats$adj_r_2_demo, 3), color = "black", bold = T, 
                              background = spec_color(lm_stats$adj_r_2_demo, option = "turbo", 
                                                  scale_from = c(min(r_2_range), max(r_2_range)),
                                                  begin = 0.2, end = 0.8))

lm_kable <- kbl(lm_stats_k, caption = "Model Performance Statistics", digits = 3,
                col.names = c("Region","Adj. R Squared", "Adj. R Sq. w/o Sutures"), escape = F) %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)

save_kable(lm_kable, 
           paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/explanatory_rsq.html"))

webshot2::webshot(url = paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/explanatory_rsq.html"),
                  file = paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/explanatory_rsq.png"))

lm_kable
```

## Coefficients on the brain

We create a brain shape to plot the results on top of the region map:
```{r brain shape}

# Include diagram from Porras paper
knitr::include_graphics(here::here("../Literature/Region_Diagram.png"))

# Manually define center points of regions
brain_points <- tribble(
  ~region, ~x, ~y,
  1, 1, 7,
  2, 2, 8, 
  3, 3, 8,
  4, 4, 7,
  5, 4, 5,
  6, 3, 4, 
  7, 4, 3,
  8, 3, 1,
  9, 2, 1,
  10, 1, 3,
  11, 2, 4,
  12, 1, 5
) %>% 
  st_as_sf(coords = c("x", "y"), remove = F)

# Create nearest-neighbor regions around these points
g = st_combine(st_geometry(brain_points)) # make multipoint
v = st_voronoi(g)
v = st_collection_extract(v)

# Add region numbers
brain_shapes <- v[unlist(st_intersects(brain_points, v))] %>% 
  st_as_sf() %>% 
  mutate(region_num = 1:n())

# Create circle around whole shape
brain_circle <- tibble(x = 2.5, y = 4.5) %>% 
  st_as_sf(coords = c("x", "y")) %>% 
  st_buffer(4.5)

# Crop nearest neighbor regions to circle
brain_shapes <- st_intersection(brain_shapes, brain_circle)
```

Next, we plot the coefficients on the map of the brain regions.

```{r coeffs}

# Extract and clean coefficients and names
reg_coeffs <- coeff_tbl %>% 
  mutate(var_clean = str_remove_all(term, "_fused") %>% 
           str_replace_all("_", " ") %>% 
           str_remove_all("\\(|\\)") %>% 
           str_to_title()) %>%
  mutate(var_clean = case_match(var_clean,
                                "Vol Dev Head Std" ~ "Actual - Ref (Std.)", 
                                "Image Typect" ~ "CT vs 3D",
                                "Pt Sexmale" ~ "Sex: Male",
                                "Age Std" ~ "Age (Std.)",
                                .default = var_clean)) %>% 
  mutate(var_clean = fct_inorder(var_clean)) %>% 
  left_join(brain_shapes) %>% 
  st_as_sf()

# Filter out bicoronal interaction term
reg_coeffs %<>% 
  filter(!term %in% c("left_coronal_fused:right_coronal_fused"))

reg_coeffs_control <- reg_coeffs %>% 
  filter(!str_detect(term, "fused|bicoronal"))

reg_coeffs_fusion <- reg_coeffs %>% 
  filter(str_detect(term, "fused|bicoronal"))

# Plot coefficients on brain shape
coeff_plot_control <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = reg_coeffs_control, 
          aes(fill = estimate)) +
  geom_sf_text(data = st_centroid(reg_coeffs_control), aes(label = region_num)) +
  facet_wrap(~var_clean, nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "turbo", 
                       limits = (c(-1, 1) * max(abs(reg_coeffs$estimate)))) +
  labs(x = "", y = "", fill = "Estimate", title = "Expected Volume Deviations: Demographics")

coeff_plot_fusion <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = reg_coeffs_fusion, aes(fill = estimate)) +
  geom_sf_text(data = st_centroid(reg_coeffs_fusion), aes(label = region_num)) +
  facet_wrap(~var_clean, nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "turbo", 
                       limits = (c(-1, 1) * max(abs(reg_coeffs$estimate)))) +
  labs(x = "", y = "", fill = "Estimate", title = "Expected Volume Deviations by Suture Type")

ggsave(paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/coefficient_plot_control.png"),
       coeff_plot_control,
       width = 8, height = 4, units = "in")

ggsave(paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/coefficient_plot_fusion.png"),
       coeff_plot_fusion,
       width = 8, height = 4, units = "in")

coeff_plot_control
coeff_plot_fusion
```

# Explanatory Model Diagnostics

## Explanatory Model Residuals

Next, we check the shape of the residuals.

```{r residuals}

# Extract residuals in dataset
resid_df <- vol_reg$residuals %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>% 
  rownames_to_column() %>% 
  pivot_longer(-rowname, values_to = "resid")

# Reshape regression data to merge to residuals
vols_df <- cranio_reg %>% 
  select(c(matches("reg_"), pt_sex, age, 
           suture_type, pt_race, pt_ethnicity,
           head_vol, ref_head_vol)) %>% 
  mutate(rowname = as.character(1:n())) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = matches("reg_"), values_to = "vol_dev")

# Merge regression data onto residuals
resid_df %<>%
  full_join(vols_df) %>% 
  mutate(region_num = str_extract(name, "[0-9]+") %>% 
           as.numeric() %>% as.factor())

# Plot 12 regions' residuals as a ridge plot
resid_ridge <- ggplot(resid_df, 
                      aes(x = resid, y = region_num, fill = region_num)) +
  geom_density_ridges() +
  labs(title = "Residuals by Region")

# Plot 12 regions' residuals as boxplot with jittered points to see outliers
resid_box <- ggplot(resid_df, 
                    aes(x = resid, y = region_num, fill = region_num)) +
  geom_jitter(width=0.1, alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Residuals by Region")

resid_ridge | resid_box

```

## Explanatory Model Correlations

We also plot the correlation matrices of the volume differences and the brain shapes of the residual correlations. Our model currently assumes that every observation's residuals follow the same distribution, but we observe that there is a different pattern in the residuals depending on the suture type.

```{r cov matrices, results = 'hide', fig.dim = c(15, 15)}

# Extract outcomes
Y <- vol_reg$model[[1]]

# Calculate coefficient correlation matrix
cor_mx <- cov2cor(vcov(vol_reg)) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>% 
  rownames_to_column() %>% 
  pivot_longer(-rowname, names_to = "colname") %>% 
  # Clean names of regions
  mutate(region_num_1 = str_extract(rowname, "[0-9]+") %>% 
           as.numeric() %>% as.factor(),
         region_num_2 = str_extract(colname, "[0-9]+") %>% 
           as.numeric() %>% as.factor(),
         variable_1 = str_match(rowname, "\\:(.*)")[,2] %>% 
           str_remove_all("suture_type") %>% 
           str_replace_all("_", " ") %>% 
           str_remove_all("\\(|\\)") %>% 
           str_to_title(),
         variable_2 = str_match(colname, "\\:(.*)")[,2] %>%  
           str_remove_all("suture_type") %>% 
           str_replace_all("_", " ") %>% 
           str_remove_all("\\(|\\)") %>% 
           str_to_title()
  )

# Identify correlation between different regions within same suture
cor_mx_suture <- cor_mx %>% 
  filter(variable_1 == variable_2 &
           variable_1 != "Intercept")

# Identify all suture types (excluding no suture)
all_suts <- setdiff(unique(sort(cranio_reg$suture_type)), "no_suture")

# Specify sutures to graph
print_set <- all_suts  # c("sagittal")

### Extract and graph correlation of observations, residuals, and coefficients
cor_y_list <- list()
cor_resid_list <- list()
cor_beta_list <- list()
cor_resid_shape_list <- list()

for(s in all_suts){
  
  # Clean suture name
  s_nm <- s %>% str_replace_all("_", " ") %>% 
    str_remove_all("\\(|\\)") %>% 
    str_to_title()
  
  # Identify set of observations with this suture
  s_set <- which(cranio_reg$suture_type == s)
  
  # Extract correlation matrix within this suture
  cor_y <- cor(Y[s_set,]) %>% 
    as.data.frame() %>% 
    rownames_to_column() %>% 
    pivot_longer(cols = -rowname) %>% 
    mutate(across(c(rowname, name), 
                  ~str_extract(., "[0-9]+") %>% as.numeric() %>% as.factor()))
  
  # Extract residual correlation matrix within this suture
  cor_resid <- cor(vol_reg$residuals[s_set,]) %>% 
    as.data.frame() %>% 
    rownames_to_column() %>% 
    pivot_longer(cols = -rowname) %>% 
    mutate(across(c(rowname, name), 
                  ~str_extract(., "[0-9]+") %>% as.numeric() %>% as.factor()))
  
  # Plot outcome correlation matrix
  cy <- ggplot(cor_y) + 
    geom_tile(aes(x = name, y = rowname, fill = value)) +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "turbo", limits = c(-1, 1)) +
    scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
    labs(title = paste("Volume Corr.:", s),
         x = "Region", y = "Region", fill = "r")
  
  # Plot residual correlation matrix
  cr <- ggplot(cor_resid) + 
    geom_tile(aes(x = name, y = rowname, fill = value)) +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "turbo", limits = c(-1, 1)) +
    scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
    labs(title = paste("Residual Corr.:", s),
         x = "Region", y = "Region", fill = "r")
  
  # Plot coefficient correlation matrix
  cor_plot_suture <- ggplot(cor_mx_suture %>% filter(variable_1 == s_nm)) +
    geom_tile(aes(x = region_num_1, y = region_num_2, fill = value)) +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "turbo", limits = c(-1, 1)) +
    scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
    labs(title = paste("Coefficient Corr.:", s),
         x = "Region", y = "Region", fill = "r")
  
  # Create residual correlations as brain shape
  resid_shapes <- cor_resid %>% 
    left_join(brain_shapes %>% 
                mutate(region_num = as.factor(region_num)), 
              by = c("name" = "region_num")) %>% 
    st_as_sf() %>% 
    mutate(is_d = if_else(name == rowname, "Yes", "No"),
           corr = if_else(name == rowname, NA_real_, value))
  
  # Plot residual correlation on brain shape
  resid_shape_plot <- ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data = resid_shapes, aes(fill = corr)) +
    geom_sf_text(data = st_centroid(resid_shapes), aes(label = name)) +
    facet_wrap(~rowname) +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "turbo", na.value = "white", limits = c(-1, 1)) +
    labs(x = "", y = "", fill = "Correlation",
         title = paste("Residual Correlation:", s))
  
  # Save lists of all these
  cor_y_list[[s]] <- cy
  cor_resid_list[[s]] <- cr
  cor_beta_list[[s]] <- cor_plot_suture
  cor_resid_shape_list[[s]] <- resid_shape_plot
  
}

# Display
wrap_plots(cor_y_list)

wrap_plots(cor_resid_shape_list, ncol = 2)
```

We plot the covariance matrix of all coefficients in the model.

```{r all cov, fig.dim = c(10, 10)}
# Extract all covariances
cor_mx_all <- cor_mx %>% 
  mutate(across(c(variable_1, variable_2),
                ~if_else(. == "Intercept", "_Intercept", .))) %>% 
  mutate(id_1 = paste0(str_pad(region_num_1, 2, "left"), "_", variable_1),
         id_2 = paste0(str_pad(region_num_2, 2, "left"), "_", variable_2))

# Plot combined correlation matrix
ggplot(cor_mx_all) +
  geom_tile(aes(x = id_1, y = id_2, fill = value)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "turbo", limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5))
```

# Predictive Model

We run a model that only includes the (107) observations that are a repeated measurement.

## Repeat Visit Data Summaries

First, we make a 2-dimensional histogram of time since previous picture and change in volume difference since previous picture.

```{r ar data, fig.dim = c(15, 15)}

# Create data that includes previous visits and difference from previous
cranio_ar <- cranio_sub %>% 
  arrange(uid, age) %>% 
  group_by(uid) %>% 
  mutate(pt_visits = n(),
         across(c(age, vol_dev_head, matches("reg_")),
                .fns = list("prev" = ~lag(., 1),
                            "diff" = ~ . - lag(., 1)))) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(across(c(head_vol, vol_dev_head, age),
                ~ ( . - cranio_scale[[paste0(cur_column(), "_mean")]][1]) / 
                  cranio_scale[[paste0(cur_column(), "_sd")]][1],
                .names = "{.col}_std"))

# Save one version including first / only visits
cranio_ar_all <- cranio_ar

# Save version for models using previous data: excludes first / only visits
cranio_ar %<>% 
  filter(!is.na(age_prev))

### Nest region-specific data sets
cranio_ar_region <- cranio_ar %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("reg_"),
               names_pattern = "(reg_[0-9]+)_(.*)",
               names_to = c("region_num", ".value")) %>% 
  group_by(region_num) %>%
  nest() %>% 
  mutate(count_plot = map2(
    data, region_num,
    ~ggplot(data = .x %>% filter(age_diff <= 365), 
            aes(x = age_diff, y = vol_dev_diff)) +
    geom_bin_2d(binwidth = c(30, 5)) +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "turbo", trans = "log", labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 1)) +
    labs(title = glue("{.y} Count"),
         x = "Time Change", y = "Change in Volume Difference")
  ))

wrap_plots(cranio_ar_region$count_plot)
```

```{r ar summ}
# Summarize counts in multiple visit data
ar_summ <- cranio_ar %>% 
  summarize("Rows" = n(),
            "Unique Pictures" = n_distinct(image_identifier),
            "Unique Patients" = n_distinct(uid)) %>% 
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "  ", values_to = " ")

kable(ar_summ, caption = "Repeat Visits Data") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)
```

```{r ar sutures}
# Summarize suture types in multiple visit data
sut_ar <- cranio_ar %>% 
  group_by(suture_type) %>% 
  summarize(pics = n(),
            patients = n_distinct(uid)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  arrange(desc(pics)) %>% 
  mutate(pats_included = if_else(suture_type %in% c("multiple", "right_lambdoid", "left_lambdoid"), 0, patients),
         pct_pats = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)(patients / sum(patients)),
         pct_included = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)(pats_included / sum(pats_included))) %>% 
  adorn_totals("row") %>% 
  select(-pats_included)

sut_kable_ar <- kable(sut_ar, caption = "Sutures: Predictive Data", digits = 2,
      col.names = c("Suture Type", "Pictures", "Patients", "% of Patients", "% of Included")) %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)

save_kable(sut_kable_ar, paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/sut_summ_ar.html"))

webshot2::webshot(url = paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/sut_summ_ar.html"),
                  file = paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/sut_summ_ar.png"))

sut_kable_ar
```

## Predictive Model by Regions

Next, we run 12 separate auto-regressive models for the 12 regions. We include every coefficient from the multivariate model, plus a tensor product interaction of previous volume difference and time since previous picture.

```{r ar model}

# Run models separately on 12 regions
cranio_ar_region %<>%
  mutate(
    # Model using first-order and interaction terms of previous outcome and time since previous outcome
    te_model = map(data, ~{
      mgcv::gam(vol_dev ~ 0 +
                  metopic_fused + sagittal_fused + left_coronal_fused + right_coronal_fused + 
                  right_coronal_fused * left_coronal_fused + 
                  image_type + pt_sex + age_std + vol_dev_head_prev +
                  te(age_diff, vol_dev_prev), 
                data = .x) }),
    # Model using previous outcome as a linear term
    prev_model = map(data, ~{
      lm(vol_dev ~ 0 +
           metopic_fused + sagittal_fused + left_coronal_fused + right_coronal_fused + 
           right_coronal_fused * left_coronal_fused + 
           image_type + pt_sex + age_std + vol_dev_head_prev + vol_dev_prev, 
         data = .x) }),
    # Model not using previous outcome
    noar_model = map(data, ~{
      lm(vol_dev ~ 0 +
           metopic_fused + sagittal_fused + left_coronal_fused + right_coronal_fused + 
           right_coronal_fused * left_coronal_fused + 
           image_type + pt_sex + age_std,
         data = .x) })
  )
```

## Predictive Model Performance

```{r ar performance}

# Generate summaries for each model; extract R Squared and Smooth P Val
ar_region_stats <- cranio_ar_region %>%
  mutate(te_model_summ = map(te_model, summary),
         prev_model_summ = map(prev_model, summary),
         noar_model_summ = map(noar_model, summary),
         te_model_rsq = map_dbl(te_model_summ, ~pluck(., "r.sq")),
         prev_model_rsq = map_dbl(prev_model_summ, ~pluck(., "r.squared")),
         noar_model_rsq = map_dbl(noar_model_summ, ~pluck(., "r.squared")),
         p_te = map_dbl(te_model_summ, ~pluck(., "s.pv")))

# Keep dataset with only numerical stats
ar_region_stats_num <- ar_region_stats %>% 
  select(matches("region_num|rsq|p_t"))

# Create Kable with nice background highlighting
ar_region_stats_k <- map(
  names(ar_region_stats_num),
  ~{
    nm <- .x
    
    if(nm %in% c("region_num")) {y <- ar_region_stats_num[[nm]]}
  
    # Color r squared based on color scale
    if(str_detect(nm, "rsq")){
      y <- cell_spec(round(ar_region_stats_num[[nm]], 3), 
                     color = "black", bold = T, 
                     background = spec_color(ar_region_stats_num[[nm]], 
                                             option = "turbo", 
                                             scale_from = c(0,1))) }
    
    # Use different color scheme for significance values
    if(str_detect(nm, "p_t")){
      y <- cell_spec(round(ar_region_stats_num[[nm]], 3), 
                     color = "black", bold = T, 
                     background = spec_color(ar_region_stats_num[[nm]], 
                                             option = "turbo",
                                             begin = 0.25, end = 0.75,
                                             scale_from = c(0, 0.05))) }
    
    return(y)
} ) %>% 
  bind_cols()

# Set names of kable
names(ar_region_stats_k) <- names(ar_region_stats_num)

# Display statistics
kbl(ar_region_stats_k, 
    caption = "Repeat Visit Performance Statistics", digits = 3,
    col.names = c("Region", 
                  "R Sq TE", "R Sq Prev No Time", "R Sq No AR", 
                  "Smooth P Val TE"), 
    escape = F) %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)

```

Most regions have significance when we include underlying interactions with "te". However, it is often the case that a model with the previous volume deviation but no time since the previous measurement, or a model with no AR term, performs comparably well. 

We plot contour plots from the splines included to model the effect of previous region-specific volume deviation from the reference head and previous overall volume deviation from the reference head, interacted with time since previous visit.

## AR Spline Interpretation

```{r gam plots}

# Single plot of contour of spline

# https://r.qcbs.ca/workshop08/pres-en/workshop08-pres-en.html#1

# for(j in 1:length(ar_list)){
#   
#   fvisgam(ar_list[[j]]$ar_ti[[1]],
#           view = c("age_diff", "vol_dev_prev"),
#           plot.type = "contour",
#           color = "topo",
#           main = "Age * Prev Region",
#           dec = 1,
#           hide.label = T) %>% 
#     print()
# }

```


```{r gam predictions}

# Define size of prediction combinations
pred_grid_n <- 30

# Add temporary row to initialize
predict_settings <- cranio_ar_region %>% 
  select(-count_plot) %>% 
  mutate(pred_data = map(data, ~{
    dt = .x
    
    y <- tibble(temp = 1:pred_grid_n) %>% 
      mutate(
      metopic_fused = 0,
      sagittal_fused = 0,
      left_coronal_fused = 0,
      right_coronal_fused = 0,
      vol_diff_overall = 0,
      image_type = "CT",
      pt_sex = "Male",
      age_std = 0
    ) %>% 
      mutate(vol_dev_head_prev = seq(from = min(cranio_ar$vol_dev_head_prev, na.rm = T),
                                     to = max(cranio_ar$vol_dev_head_prev, na.rm = T),
                                     length.out = pred_grid_n)) %>% 
      mutate(
        age_diff = list(seq(from = min(cranio_ar$age_diff, na.rm = T),
                            to = max(cranio_ar$age_diff, na.rm = T), 
                            length.out = pred_grid_n))) %>% 
      mutate(vol_dev_prev = seq(from = min(dt$vol_dev_prev, na.rm = T),
                                to = max(dt$vol_dev_prev, na.rm = T),
                                length.out = pred_grid_n)) %>% 
      unnest_longer(age_diff)
    
    return(y)
  }))
```

```{r gam predict plots}

# Predict AR models on 12 regions and identify spline z-scores
predict_settings %<>%
  mutate(
    te_pred = map2(pred_data, te_model,
                   ~data.frame(
                     predict(.y, newdata = .x, 
                             type = "terms", se.fit = T)))) %>% 
  # Create data with predictions, standard errors, z-scores, and original data
  mutate(te_pred_mod = map2(
    te_pred, pred_data,
    ~rename(.x, 
             te_age_region = fit.te.age_diff.vol_dev_prev.,
             te_age_region_se = se.fit.te.age_diff.vol_dev_prev.) %>% 
      mutate(te_age_region_sig = te_age_region / te_age_region_se) %>% 
      bind_cols(.y %>% select(matches("prev|diff")))
    ))

# Save min and max z score for consistent coloring
color_lims <- c(
  min(map_dbl(predict_settings$te_pred_mod, ~min(.$te_age_region_sig))),
  max(map_dbl(predict_settings$te_pred_mod, ~max(.$te_age_region_sig)))
)

# Create spline plots
predict_settings %<>%
  mutate(te_sig_plot = map2(te_pred_mod, region_num, ~{
    ggplot(.x, aes(x = age_diff, 
                   y = vol_dev_prev, 
                   fill = te_age_region_sig)) + 
      geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = color_lims, option = "turbo") +
  # geom_text(aes(label = round(te_age_region_sig, 1)), size = 2) +
  labs(title = .y,
       fill = "TE / SE",
       x = "Time",
       y = "Previous")
  }))

# Display all 12 region spline plots
ggarrange(plotlist = predict_settings$te_sig_plot,
          common.legend = T, legend = "bottom")

g_splines <- gridExtra::arrangeGrob(grobs = predict_settings$te_sig_plot,
          common.legend = T, legend = "bottom")

ggsave(file = paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/region_splines.png"),
       g_splines,
       width = 10, height = 8, units = "in")
```

# Predictive Model: Combined Spline Estimate

We run one auto-regressive model in which all regions are used together to estimate the spline. We create region-specific covariates, so all covariates other than the spline will still be trained on each region separately.

```{r combo df}
# Create wider data with interactions between covariates and regions
cranio_ar_comb <- cranio_ar %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("reg_"),
               names_pattern = "(reg_[0-9]+)_(.*)",
               names_to = c("region_num", ".value")) %>% 
  mutate(image_type = as.numeric(image_type == "CT"),
         pt_sex = as.numeric(pt_sex == "Male")) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = region_num,
              values_from = c(matches("fused"), 
                              image_type, pt_sex, age_std, vol_dev_head_prev), 
              names_glue = "{region_num}_{.value}", 
              values_fill = 0) %>% 
  select(matches("reg_"), vol_dev_prev, vol_dev, age_diff) %>% 
  select(-matches("lambdoid"))

```

```{r combo model}
# List all region-specific covariates plus combined spline in one model
reg_form_combo <- formula(
    paste0("vol_dev ~ 0 + te(age_diff, vol_dev_prev) + ",
           paste(names(cranio_ar_comb %>% 
                         select(matches("reg_"))),
                 collapse = " + "), " + ",
           paste(paste0("reg_", 1:12, 
                        "_left_coronal_fused * reg_", 1:12, 
                        "_right_coronal_fused"), collapse = " + ")
    ))

# Run combination model
reg_ar_combo <- mgcv::gam(reg_form_combo, 
                    data = cranio_ar_comb)


```

## Combined Predictive Model Performance

```{r ar combo performance}

### Create model performance statistics
ar_combo <- tibble(
  adj_r_sq_combo = summary(reg_ar_combo)$r.sq,
  p_te_combo = summary(reg_ar_combo)$s.pv) %>%
  # Summarize how combined model performance compares to region-specific
  mutate(rsq_pctile_te = mean(ar_region_stats_num$te_model_rsq <= adj_r_sq_combo),
         rsq_pctile_prev = mean(ar_region_stats_num$prev_model_rsq <= adj_r_sq_combo),
         rsq_pctile_lin = mean(ar_region_stats_num$noar_model_rsq <= adj_r_sq_combo))

# Display performance statistics
kbl(ar_combo, caption = "Combined Spline Statistics", digits = 3,
    col.names = c("R Sq", "Smooth P Val (TE)", 
                  "R Sq Pctile vs TE", 
                  "R Sq Pctile vs Prev", "R Sq Pctile vs Lin"), 
    escape = F) %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)

```

## Combined Spline Plots

```{r combo spline}
# fvisgam(reg_ar_combo,
#         view = c("age_diff", "vol_dev_prev"),
#         plot.type = "contour",
#         color = "topo",
#         main = "Age * Prev Region",
#         dec = 1,
#         hide.label = T)
```

```{r gam predictions combo}

# Define size of prediction combinations
pred_grid_n <- 30

# Add temporary row to initialize
predict_settings_combo <- tibble(temp = c(1:pred_grid_n))

# Add columns of previous outcome ranges
predict_settings_combo %<>% 
  mutate(
    vol_dev_prev = seq(from = min(cranio_ar_comb$vol_dev_prev, na.rm = T), 
                       to = max(cranio_ar_comb$vol_dev_prev, na.rm = T), 
                       length.out = pred_grid_n),
    age_diff = list(seq(from = min(cranio_ar_comb$age_diff, na.rm = T),
                        to = max(cranio_ar_comb$age_diff, na.rm = T), length.out = pred_grid_n))
  )

# Add other columns, with 0 as default

for(nm in names(cranio_ar_comb)){
  
  if(nm %in% names(predict_settings_combo)){ next }
  
  predict_settings_combo[[nm]] <- 0
  
}

# Expand grid
predict_settings_combo %<>% unnest_longer(age_diff)
```

```{r gam predict plots combo}

# Create data set of predictions and sample data
te_combo_pred <- data.frame(predict(reg_ar_combo, 
                                newdata = predict_settings_combo, 
                                type = "terms", se.fit = T)) %>% 
    rename(te_age = fit.te.age_diff.vol_dev_prev.,
           te_age_se = se.fit.te.age_diff.vol_dev_prev.) %>% 
    mutate(te_age_sig = te_age / te_age_se) %>% 
    bind_cols(predict_settings_combo %>% select(matches("prev|diff")))
  
# Plot predictions on grid of values going into the spline
te_plot_combo <- ggplot(te_combo_pred, aes(x = age_diff, 
                                           y = vol_dev_prev, 
                                           fill = te_age_sig)) + 
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "turbo") +
  # geom_text(aes(label = round(te_age_region_sig, 1)), size = 2) +
  labs(title = "Combined Spline",
       fill = "TE / SE")

# Display combined model plot
te_plot_combo

ggsave(paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/combo_splines.png"),
       te_plot_combo,
       width = 10, height = 8, units = "in")
```

# Cross-Validated Predictions

We create a fresh version of the "AR" data, including information from previous visits, in order to keep this data not reliant on the above sections.

```{r cv data}
# Create new data with previous values for cross-validated model
cranio_cv <- cranio_sub %>% 
  # Enforce order of patient IDs
  arrange(uid, age) %>% 
  group_by(uid) %>% 
  mutate(pt_visits = n(),
         across(c(age, vol_dev_head, matches("reg_")),
                .fns = list("prev" = ~lag(., 1),
                            "diff" = ~ . - lag(., 1)))) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(across(c(head_vol, vol_dev_head, age),
                ~ ( . - cranio_scale[[paste0(cur_column(), "_mean")]][1]) / 
                  cranio_scale[[paste0(cur_column(), "_sd")]][1],
                .names = "{.col}_std"))

# Limit data to one observation per patient (all patients)
cranio_cv_1per <- cranio_cv %>% 
  group_by(uid) %>% 
  # Use last picture taken for each patient
  slice_tail(n=1) %>% 
  ungroup()

# Keep patients with multiple visits
cranio_cv_mult <- cranio_cv %>% 
  filter(!is.na(age_prev)) %>% 
  # Exclude only observation of their suture type
  group_by(suture_type) %>% 
  mutate(n_obs = n()) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  filter(n_obs > 1) %>% 
  select(-n_obs)

# Create wider data with interactions between covariates and regions
cranio_cv_comb <- cranio_cv_mult %>%
  filter(!is.na(age_prev)) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("reg_"),
               names_pattern = "(reg_[0-9]+)_(.*)",
               names_to = c("region", ".value")) %>% 
  mutate(image_type = as.numeric(image_type == "CT"),
         pt_sex = as.numeric(pt_sex == "Male"),
         region2 = region) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = region,
              values_from = c(matches("fused"), 
                              image_type, pt_sex, age_std, vol_dev_head_prev), 
              names_glue = "{region}_{.value}", 
              values_fill = 0) %>% 
  select(uid, age, region = region2,
         matches("reg_"), vol_dev_prev, vol_dev, age_diff) %>% 
  select(-matches("lambdoid"))

```

We run a loop estimating several models while excluding one patient who has multiple visits. Then, we predict using that model on the excluded patient to determine that model's overall accuracy. 

```{r leave one out}

### Initialize loop

# Save set of patients with multiple visits
subj_ids_mult <- unique(cranio_cv_mult$uid)

# Count patients with multiple visits
n_subj_mult <- length(subj_ids_mult)

# - Mean Sq Pred Error
# - Prediction plots (brain shape for all 88)

# Initialize results data
CV_results <- tibble(NULL)

subj_i <- 1
for(subj_i in 1:n_subj_mult){
  
  # Pull current subject to be left out
  cur_subj <- subj_ids_mult[subj_i]
  
  # Drop subject from all data
  cranio_cv_drop_i <- cranio_cv_1per %>% 
    filter(uid != cur_subj)
  
  # Drop subject from only repeat visits
  cranio_cv_mult_drop_i <- cranio_cv_mult %>% 
    filter(!is.na(age_prev))
  
  # Drop subject for combined model data
  cranio_cv_comb_drop_i <- cranio_cv_comb %>% 
    filter(uid != cur_subj)
  
  # Keep separate data of dropped subject
  cranio_cv_i <- cranio_cv %>% 
    filter(uid == cur_subj) %>%
    arrange(age) %>%
    mutate(visit = row_number()) %>%
    filter(visit == 2)
  
  # Keep separate combined data of dropped subject
  cranio_cv_comb_i <- cranio_cv_comb %>% 
    filter(uid == cur_subj) %>%
    # Keep 2nd visit (1st in dataset, since 1st is already excluded above)
    filter(age == min(age))
    
  ### Fit models:
  
  # 1) Data of last / only visits (model not relying on previous)
  all_pts_models_drop_i <- cranio_cv_drop_i %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("reg_"),
                 names_pattern = "(reg_[0-9]+)_(.*)",
                 names_to = c("region", ".value")) %>%
    group_by(region) %>%
    nest() %>%
    # Linear model
    mutate(
      simple_all_model = map(data, 
                         ~lm(vol_dev ~ 0 +
                               metopic_fused + sagittal_fused + 
                               left_coronal_fused + right_coronal_fused + 
                               right_coronal_fused * left_coronal_fused + 
                               image_type + pt_sex + age_std,
                             data = .x)))
  
  # 2) Data excluding first / only visits (models relying on previous visits)
  rpt_pts_models_drop_i <- cranio_cv_mult_drop_i %>% 
    pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("reg_"),
                 names_pattern = "(reg_[0-9]+)_(.*)",
                 names_to = c("region", ".value")) %>%
    group_by(region) %>%
    nest() %>%
    mutate(
      simple_rpt_model = map(data, 
                             ~lm(vol_dev ~ 0 +
                                   metopic_fused + sagittal_fused + 
                                   left_coronal_fused + right_coronal_fused + 
                                   right_coronal_fused * left_coronal_fused + 
                                   image_type + pt_sex + age_std,
                                 data = .x)),
      # Linear model using previous observations for region and overall
      prev_model = map(data,
                       ~lm(vol_dev ~ 0 +
                             metopic_fused + sagittal_fused + 
                             left_coronal_fused + right_coronal_fused + 
                             right_coronal_fused * left_coronal_fused + 
                             image_type + pt_sex + age_std + 
                             vol_dev_head_prev + vol_dev_prev, 
                           data = .x)),
      # GAM using linear previous overall and surface of previous region
      te_model = map(data,
                     ~mgcv::gam(vol_dev ~ 0 +
                                  metopic_fused + sagittal_fused + 
                                  left_coronal_fused + right_coronal_fused + 
                                  right_coronal_fused * left_coronal_fused + 
                                  image_type + pt_sex + age_std + 
                                  vol_dev_head_prev +
                                  te(age_diff, vol_dev_prev), 
                                data = .x))
    )
  
  # 3) "Combined" data: excluding first / only visits, with region interactions
  comb_model_drop_i <- mgcv::gam(reg_form_combo, 
                                 data = cranio_cv_comb_drop_i)
  
  # Get region predictions for excluded observation
  region_models_pred <- cranio_cv_i %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("reg_"),
                 names_pattern = "(reg_[0-9]+)_(.*)",
                 names_to = c("region", ".value")) %>%
    group_by(region) %>%
    nest(.key = "new_sample") %>%
    # Merge models estimated without observation
    left_join(all_pts_models_drop_i, join_by(region)) %>% 
    rename(all_pts_data = data) %>% 
    left_join(rpt_pts_models_drop_i, join_by(region)) %>% 
    mutate(simple_all_pred = map2_dbl(simple_all_model, new_sample, 
                                      ~predict(.x, newdata = .y)),
           simple_rpt_pred = map2_dbl(simple_rpt_model, new_sample, 
                                      ~predict(.x, newdata = .y)),
           prev_pred = map2_dbl(prev_model, new_sample, 
                                ~predict(.x, newdata = .y)),
           te_pred = map2_dbl(te_model, new_sample, 
                              ~predict(.x, newdata = .y))) %>%
    hoist(new_sample, "vol_dev")
  
  # Predict combined model on 1 observation's 12 outcomes
  cranio_cv_comb_i$combo_pred <- predict(comb_model_drop_i,
                                         newdata = cranio_cv_comb_i)
  
  # Compile original values and predictions from every model
  pred_df_i <- region_models_pred %>% 
    select(region, vol_dev, matches("_pred")) %>% 
    ungroup() %>% 
    # Merge predictions from combined model
    left_join(cranio_cv_comb_i %>% 
                select(region, combo_pred),
              join_by(region)) %>% 
    # Calculate out of sample accuracy
    mutate(across(matches("_pred"),
                  ~ (vol_dev - .)^2,
                  .names = "{.col}_sqerr"))
  
  # Add to running total of results
  new_rows <- pred_df_i %>% 
    mutate(
      dropped_subject = subj_i,
      dropped_subject_id = cur_subj,
    )
  
  # Save in full results data
  CV_results <- bind_rows(CV_results, new_rows)
}

```

## CV Prediction Summaries

We summarize the distributions of square errors from these predictions. We want to look at how the various models may perform better or worse across suture types and individual regions.

```{r summarize mse}

# Save info about patients to look for patterns
patient_info <- cranio_cv_1per %>% 
  select(uid, pt_sex, pt_race, pt_ethnicity, suture_type)

# Count number of observations of each suture type
sut_counts_cv <- cranio_cv_mult %>% 
  group_by(suture_type) %>% 
  summarize(sut_obs = n()) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(sut_pct = sut_obs / sum(sut_obs))


# Merge this info to predictions
cv_out <- CV_results %>% 
  left_join(patient_info, by = c("dropped_subject_id" = "uid")) %>% 
  left_join(sut_counts_cv, by = "suture_type") %>% 
  mutate(region_num = as.numeric(str_extract(region, "[0-9]+")))

# Pivot so that model name is in a column
cv_out_long <- cv_out %>% 
  rename(vol_dev_pred = vol_dev) %>% 
  pivot_longer(matches("_pred"), 
               names_pattern = "(.*)_(pred.*)",
               names_to = c("model", ".value"))

# Create pretty names for models
cv_out_long %<>%
  mutate(model = factor(model, levels = c("vol_dev", "simple_all", "simple_rpt", "prev", "te", "combo"))) %>% 
  arrange(model) %>% 
  mutate(model_neat = case_match(model, 
                                 "vol_dev" ~ "Actual",
                                 "simple_all" ~ "No Past Visits (All Data)",
                                 "simple_rpt" ~ "No Past Visits",
                                 "prev" ~ "Prev. Visit Linear",
                                 "te" ~ "Prev. Visit * Time",
                                 "combo" ~ "Prev. Visit * Time Combo")) %>% 
  mutate(model_neat = fct_inorder(model_neat))

# Summarize square errors by model by suture
sqerr_summ <- cv_out_long %>% 
  filter(!model %in% c("vol_dev", "simple_all")) %>% 
  # Average across regions to get one square error per patient
  group_by(dropped_subject, dropped_subject_id, 
           suture_type, sut_obs, sut_pct, model_neat) %>%
  summarize(sqerr = mean(pred_sqerr)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  # Summarize distributions of square errors by suture by model
  group_by(suture_type, sut_obs, sut_pct,
           model_neat) %>% 
  summarize(mean = mean(sqerr),
            quant = list(quantile(sqerr, 
                                  c(0.05, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.95),
                                  names = T))) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  unnest_wider(quant, names_sep = "_", names_repair = "universal") %>% 
  # Sort by frequency and by best median square error
  arrange(desc(sut_obs), quant_50.)
  
# Display results
kbl(sqerr_summ, 
    caption = "Cross-Validated Square Error Summary",
    digits = 3,
    col.names = c("Suture", "Suture Count", "Suture Pct",
                  "Model",
                  "Mean Sq. Error", "5th Pctile", "25th Pctile", "Median", "75th Pctile", "95th Pctile")) %>% 
  kable_styling()

# Summarize by model
sqerr_summ_model <- sqerr_summ %>% 
  group_by(model_neat) %>% 
  summarize(model_med = weighted.mean(quant_50., sut_obs)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  arrange(model_med)

# Plot by region by suture
cv_out_long %>% 
  filter(model != "vol_dev") %>% 
  ggplot() +
  geom_jitter(aes(x = model, y = pred_sqerr, 
                  color = suture_type), 
              alpha = 0.25, width = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~region_num) +
  scale_y_continuous(transform = "log", labels = ~round(., 3)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Boxplot by region
cv_out_long %>% 
  filter(model != "vol_dev") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = interaction(model, suture_type), 
             y = pred_sqerr, 
             fill = model)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(5.5, 10.5, 15.5)) +
  scale_y_continuous(transform = "log", labels = ~round(., 3)) +
  facet_wrap(~region_num)  +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# No regions: keep suture together
suture_box <- cv_out_long %>% 
  filter(!model %in% c("vol_dev", "simple_all")) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = model_neat, 
             y = pred_sqerr, 
             fill = model_neat)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~suture_type, nrow = 1) +
  scale_y_continuous(transform = "log", labels = ~round(., 3)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom") +
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = "Out of Sample Prediction Error",
       fill = "Model")

ggsave(file = paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/suture_sq_error.png"),
       suture_box,
       width = 7, height = 5, units = "in")

suture_box

# No regions: keep model together
model_box <- cv_out_long %>% 
  filter(model != "vol_dev") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = suture_type, 
             y = pred_sqerr, 
             fill = suture_type)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~model_neat, nrow = 1, labeller = label_wrap_gen(10)) +
  scale_y_continuous(transform = "log", labels = ~round(., 3)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom") +
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = "Out of Sample Prediction Error",
       fill = "Suture")

ggsave(file = paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/model_sq_error.png"),
       model_box,
       width = 7, height = 5, units = "in")

model_box

```

We also plot a sample of predictions on top of the brain shapes. For each patient, we again take an average of the square errors across all regions for each model. Then, we identify the patients who represent the best or worst overall predictions for each model. For each of those patients, we plot the actual volume deviations and the predictions from every model. Green text and lines indicates the best prediction for a given suture type; pink represents the worst prediction for a given suture type.

```{r plot predictions, fig.dim=c(10,10)}

# Identify best and worst predictions for each suture
pred_eg <- cv_out_long %>% 
  filter(!model %in% c("simple_all")) %>% 
  group_by(dropped_subject, model) %>% 
  # Average prediction err across regions for each subject and model
  mutate(subj_model_avg_err = mean(pred_sqerr, na.rm = T)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  group_by(suture_type) %>% 
  # Maximum and minimum error for each suture type
  mutate(sut_min_err = min(subj_model_avg_err, na.rm = T),
         sut_max_err = max(subj_model_avg_err, na.rm = T)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(is_min = if_else(is.na(subj_model_avg_err) | 
                            subj_model_avg_err != sut_min_err, 0, 1),
         is_max = if_else(is.na(subj_model_avg_err) | 
                            subj_model_avg_err != sut_max_err, 0, 1)) %>% 
  mutate(example_cat = case_when(is_min == 1 ~ "Best",
                                 is_max == 1 ~ "Worst",
                                 T ~ "Neither") %>% 
           factor(levels = c("Best", "Worst", "Neither"))) %>% 
  # Identify which patient was the min / max
  group_by(dropped_subject) %>% 
  filter(sum(is_min) > 0 | sum(is_max) > 0) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  # Add brain shapes
  left_join(brain_shapes) %>% 
  st_as_sf() %>% 
  # Identify why this patient was included
  mutate(obs_label = case_when(is_min == 1 ~ paste("Min", suture_type, model),
                               is_max == 1 ~ paste("min", suture_type, model))) %>% 
  group_by(dropped_subject) %>% 
  mutate(subj_label = paste(unique(na.omit(obs_label)), collapse = "; "))

pred_range <- c(-1, 1) * max(abs(cv_out_long$pred))

# Plot predictions for each model for each of these examples
pred_plot <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = pred_eg, aes(fill = pred, color = example_cat)) +
  geom_sf_text(data = st_centroid(pred_eg), 
               aes(label = region_num, color = example_cat)) +
  facet_grid(suture_type + dropped_subject ~ model_neat,
             switch = "y",
             labeller = label_wrap_gen(10)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "turbo", limits = pred_range) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Best" = "darkgreen",
                                "Worst" = "mediumvioletred",
                                "Neither" = "black")) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, 
       fill = "Prediction", color = "Suture Pred Cat") +
  theme(axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        strip.text.y = element_text(angle = 90))

pred_plot

ggsave(file = paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/prediction_grid.png"),
       pred_plot,
       height = 8, width = 7, units = "in")

pred_eg_lone <- pred_eg %>% filter(dropped_subject == 32)

pred_plot_lone <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = pred_eg_lone, aes(fill = pred, color = example_cat)) +
  geom_sf_text(data = st_centroid(pred_eg_lone), 
               aes(label = region_num, color = example_cat)) +
  facet_grid(suture_type + dropped_subject ~ model_neat,
             switch = "y",
             labeller = label_wrap_gen(10)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "turbo", limits = pred_range) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Best" = "darkgreen",
                                "Worst" = "mediumvioletred",
                                "Neither" = "black")) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, 
       fill = "Prediction", color = "Suture Pred Cat") +
  theme(axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        strip.text.y = element_text(angle = 90))

ggsave(file = paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/prediction_grid_solo.png"),
       pred_plot_lone,
       height = 4, width = 8, units = "in")

```
